title: biscutella longifolia subsp. tirolensis
intro: Biscutella longifolia subsp. tirolensis, also known as the Alpen-Kesel or Tirolian biscutella, is a plant species in the family Brassicaceae, characterized by its small yellow flowers and narrow, pointed leaves. The medicinal properties of this plant include its ability to act as a diuretic and anti-inflammatory agent, which makes it useful for treating conditions such as kidney stones and arthritis. The health benefits of Biscutella longifolia subsp. tirolensis include its potential to improve cardiovascular health, reduce blood pressure, and alleviate symptoms of menopause. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal and health benefits.
